title: delphinium peregrinum var. halteratum
intro: Delphinium peregrinum var. halteratum, also known as the Himalayan blue poppy or blue monk's hood, is a perennial plant species in the family Ranunculaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids, glycosides, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Delphinium peregrinum var. halteratum include its potential use in treating respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, although caution should be exercised due to the plant's toxic alkaloids and potential interactions with medications.
